frameworks.name: sensory evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: gradual integration of noisy sensory evidence over seconds to support decisions

frameworks.name: intrinsic cortical timescale hierarchy
frameworks.one_line: cortical areas span a hierarchy of intrinsic integration windows

frameworks.name: distributed cortical computation
frameworks.one_line: evidence-accumulation is supported by many areas with distinct temporal windows

core_question: Are the cortical areas recruited during evidence accumulation organized by their intrinsic integration timescales?
key_assumptions: cortical areas have stable intrinsic integration timescales
decisions in this task require gradual accrual of sensory evidence
brief optogenetic inactivation reveals causal contributions
manipulations: optogenetic silencing of many cortical areas
timing of inactivation across distinct trial epochs
virtual-reality navigation task requiring seconds-long accumulation
key_findings: inactivation of all tested areas affected the evidence-accumulation computation
frontal inactivations produced stronger deficits on long timescales than posterior ones
some frontal areas also moderately affected behavior beyond accumulation
large-scale cortical Ca2+ activity showed different temporal integration windows
interpretations: the intrinsic timescale hierarchy is an important component of evidence-accumulation mechanisms
distinct cortical areas contribute to accumulation at distinct timescales
